alternative_parents.description: Organic aromatic compounds that contain a naphthalene moiety that carries a sulfonic acid group at the 1-position. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: 1-naphthalene sulfonates

alternative_parents.description: Organic aromatic compounds that contain a naphthalene moiety that carries a sulfonic acid group (or a derivative thereof) at the 1-position. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: 1-naphthalene sulfonic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic aromatic compounds that contain a naphthalene moiety that carries a sulfonic acid group at the 2-position. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: 2-naphthalene sulfonates

alternative_parents.description: Organic aromatic compounds that contain a naphthalene moiety that carries a sulfonic acid group (or a derivative thereof) at the 2-position. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: 2-naphthalene sulfonic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a sulfonic acid or a derivative thereof that is linked to a benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: Benzenesulfonic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: An arylsulfonic acid carrying the sulfonic acid group at the 1-position, and no substitute at the 2-position of the aromatic ring.
alternative_parents.name: 1-sulfo,2-unsubstituted aromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing a benzenesulfonyl group, which consists of a monocyclic benzene moiety that carries a sulfonyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Benzenesulfonyl compounds

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing a 1,3,5-triazine ring which is 2,4-disusbtituted wit amine groups.
alternative_parents.name: 1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diamines

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing a 1,3,5-triazine ring that is substituted at the 2-position with a chlorine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Chloro-s-triazines

alternative_parents.description: Primary amines where the only hydrocarbyl substituent of the amine group is aromatic. Primary amines have the general formula RNH2 (R=aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Primary aromatic amines

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the acyl chloride functional group.
alternative_parents.name: Aryl chlorides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing the sulfonyl group, with the general structure RS(=O)2R' ( R,R' must not be H).
alternative_parents.name: Sulfonyls

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing the sulfonic acid group, which has the general structure RS(=O)2OH (R is not a hydrogen atom).
alternative_parents.name: Organosulfonic acids

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an aromatic ring where a carbon atom is linked to an hetero atom.
alternative_parents.name: Heteroaromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of diazene(diimide), HN=NH, wherein both hydrogens are substituted by hydrocarbyl groups, e.g. PhN=NPh azobenzene or diphenyldiazene.
alternative_parents.name: Azo compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing metals and halogens. Some are ionic while others are covalently bonded.
alternative_parents.name: Organic metal halides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic 1,3-dipolar compounds with the general structure  X#N+-Z- <-> X-=N+=Z <-> X-=N-Z+ <-> X-N=Z (X = C or O, Z = C, N, or O).
alternative_parents.name: Propargyl-type 1,3-dipolar organic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a sodium ion.
alternative_parents.name: Organic sodium salts

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a chemical bond between a carbon atom and a chlorine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organochlorides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds with a positive electric charge.
alternative_parents.name: Organic cations

class.description: Compounds containing a naphthalene moiety, which consists of two fused benzene rings.
class.name: Naphthalenes

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as 1,1'-azonaphthalenes. These are organonitrogen aromatic compounds that contain a central azo group, where each nitrogen atom is conjugated to the 1-position of a naphthalene ring system. Naphthalene is a compound made up of two fused benzene rings.

direct_parent.description: Organonitrogen aromatic compounds that contain a central azo group, where each nitrogen atom is conjugated to the 1-position of a naphthalene ring system. Naphthalene is a compound made up of two fused benzene rings.
direct_parent.name: 1,1'-azonaphthalenes

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Organonitrogen aromatic compounds that contain a central azo group, where each nitrogen atom is conjugated to the 1-position of a naphthalene ring system. Naphthalene is a compound made up of two fused benzene rings.
subclass.name: 1,1'-azonaphthalenes

superclass.description: Aromatic compounds containing one or more benzene rings.
superclass.name: Benzenoids
